mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS below 1%) signals persistently accommodative monetary policy that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 in snapshot indicating near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that leads investors to reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in nonfarm payrolls growth (PAYEMS) alongside contracting industrial production (INDPRO) signals a broad-based real economy slowdown that is not visible from either series in isolation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142288 and INDPRO at 107.0793 in snapshot
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in PAYEMS and INDPRO is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone (interaction Rank IC < single-series Rank IC).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS), a simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals that higher rates are suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand, amplifying the negative equity market reaction relative to either signal alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3+ consecutive months AND HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise regime produces flat or positive 60-day SPX returns in over 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect is not distinguishable from the FEDFUNDS-only effect at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above trend signals rising inflationary pressure that increases the probability of future monetary tightening, causing short-horizon negative repricing of equities over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds trailing 12-month average by more than 1 standard deviation
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative bias (p > 0.05) following CPI acceleration events, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals robust consumer demand momentum that supports corporate revenue growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during RSAFS growth-triggered periods are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which correlates with short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors extrapolate near-term earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive association with INDPRO trend direction (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, the combination signals a confirmed macro downturn rather than a transient soft patch, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that are stronger than either indicator predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over same period
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16324.3 and UNRATE at 5.1 in snapshot
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1 deceleration and UNRATE rise events are followed by flat or positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect does not outperform the single-series GDPC1 model in out-of-sample Rank IC.
